name: Sourdough Dresden-Style German Stollen
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Candied orange peel, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small pan, combine milk, butter, and sugar. Place over medium heat and bring mixture to scalding, stirring to dissolve the sugar.
2. Set aside to cool.
3. Beat eggs into the cooled mixture; add this to the 2 cups of sourdough starter. Beat until well blended.
4. Add salt, almond flavoring, lemon peel, and 3 cups of flour. Mix until well-blended.
5. Stir in orange peel, raisins, currants, and almonds. Add enough flour to knead.
6. Turn dough onto a floured board and knead until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes.
7. Turn dough into a greased bowl. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size.
8. Punch dough down; divide in half.
9. On a lightly greased baking sheet, shape each half of the dough into an oval, approximately 7x9 inches and about 1 inch thick.
10. Brush surface with some egg-white mixture, then crease ovals and fold them lengthwise, not all the way over, leaving a 2-inch lap on the bottom.
11. Brush top with egg-white mixture.
12. Cover and let rise until doubled in size.
13. Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F for 40 minutes or until richly browned.
14. Brush top with melted butter and sift powdered sugar over top.
15. Return Stollens to the oven and bake an additional 3 minutes longer to set the sugar.
16. Cool on wire racks.
Take sourdough starter out of refrigerator the night before and mix with 2 cups water, 2 cups flour, and 1 tablespoon sugar. Cover. Let set overnight. Next morning, put original amount of starter back in refrigerator and use the rest for the stollen.
